```python
# test_count_vowels.py
import pytest

from your_module import count_vowels   # replace `your_module` with the real module name


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Happy-path & edge-case tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text, expected",
    [
        ("Hello World", 3),              # typical mixed-case string
        ("aeiouAEIOU", 10),              # all vowels, both cases
        ("rhythm", 0),                   # no vowels at all
        ("", 0),                         # empty string
        ("PyTh0n 3.8!", 1),              # numbers / punctuation ignored
        ("café", 1),                     # accented char not counted
        ("a" * 1_000, 1_000),            # very long input
    ],
)
def test_count_vowels_happy_and_edge_cases(text, expected):
    assert count_vowels(text) == expected


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Error-case tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [None, 123, ["a", "e"]])
def test_count_vowels_invalid_type_raises_attribute_error(bad_input):
    """
    The implementation calls `.lower()`, so any non-string argument that does
    not implement `lower` should raise `AttributeError`.
    """
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        count_vowels(bad_input)
```
